title: CareFusion AVEA Ventilator - Flow Sensor Calibration Errors
description: This guide assists in troubleshooting flow sensor calibration errors on a CareFusion AVEA. Errors can prevent accurate tidal volume and flow measurements.

steps.title: Verify Power and Startup
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is plugged into a verified power source and turned on properly. Check that all system self-tests pass at startup. Why: Flow sensor calibration cannot proceed if the device experiences startup errors or unstable power.

steps.title: Check Breathing Circuit Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm all patient circuit components are correctly installed: inspiratory/expiratory limbs, humidifier, filters, and connectors. Ensure there are no kinks, leaks, or obstructions. Why: Misconnected circuits or leaks can prevent proper flow measurement and calibration.

steps.title: Inspect the Flow Sensor Module
steps.instructions: Verify the flow sensor is seated correctly in the module port. Look for visible damage, moisture, or condensation inside the sensor. Swap with a known-good flow sensor if available. Why: External damage or poor connection is a common cause of calibration errors.

steps.title: Check Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure ambient temperature and humidity are within ventilator specifications. Remove any excessive moisture, condensation, or aerosol buildup near the sensor. Why: Flow sensors rely on precise measurements; extreme conditions can affect readings.

steps.title: Run Flow Sensor Calibration
steps.instructions: Initiate the ventilator’s calibration routine following CareFusion AVEA prompts. Observe any error codes or alarms during calibration. Why: Properly running the calibration routine can identify whether the error is persistent or intermittent.

steps.title: Inspect for Software or Configuration Issues
steps.instructions: Verify ventilator software is up to date. Confirm patient mode and circuit settings match the sensor type. Why: Incorrect configuration can prevent successful calibration.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displays flow sensor calibration error during pre-use check.
CCR.Cause: Flow sensor misaligned, moisture present, or circuit setup error.
CCR.Resolution: Verified circuit and sensor, replaced with known-good sensor, recalibrated successfully. If unsuccessful, ventilator removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Final status (operational or out of service)

title: CareFusion AVEA Ventilator - Exhalation Valve Failure
description: This guide assists in troubleshooting exhalation valve failures on a CareFusion AVEA ventilator. Exhalation valve issues can prevent proper expiration of patient breaths, trigger alarms, and compromise ventilation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Move the patient to an alternative ventilator or manual ventilation. Disconnecting or opening the exhalation valve on an active patient can be dangerous.

steps.title: Inspect the Exhalation Valve Assembly
steps.instructions: Remove the expiratory circuit and visually check for debris, condensation, or obstruction. Expected Outcome: Valve should move freely without sticking. Obstructions often cause failures.

steps.title: Check Valve Alignment and Seating
steps.instructions: Ensure the valve is properly seated in the ventilator and circuit. Misalignment can prevent proper opening/closing.

steps.title: Verify Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm expiratory limb is properly connected, and no kinks or disconnections exist. Expected Outcome: Secure connections prevent false valve alarms.

steps.title: Inspect for Condensation or Moisture
steps.instructions: Check for water or moisture accumulation in the expiratory limb or valve chamber. Drain or replace circuit components as needed. Moisture can impede valve movement.

steps.title: Check Valve Sensor Functionality (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Some AVEA models use sensors to detect valve position. Ensure connectors are secure and no visible damage exists. Expected Outcome: Valve position is properly detected; alarm resolves if sensor was the issue.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Ventilator
steps.instructions: Turn off and then back on the ventilator after checking the valve. Expected Outcome: Clears transient faults and resets valve sensing.

steps.title: Replace Disposable Components
steps.instructions: Swap the expiratory valve or circuit with a known good spare if previous steps fail. Expected Outcome: If failure resolves, the issue was with the component.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarmed with exhalation valve failure during routine use.
CCR.Cause: Valve found obstructed with condensation and partially misaligned in expiratory limb.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction, reseated valve, replaced circuit, and verified proper function; ventilator returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Valve movement before and after cleaning
Any unusual sounds or resistance during exhalation

title: CareFusion AVEA Ventilator - Pre-Use Check Failures
description: This guide assists in troubleshooting pre-use check failures on a CareFusion AVEA. Failures during pre-use checks can prevent safe patient ventilation, trigger alarms, and delay clinical care.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is not in use on a patient. Move any connected patient to an alternative ventilator if necessary.

steps.title: Check Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions: Verify the ventilator is connected to a functional power outlet. Ensure the battery is sufficiently charged if running on battery. Expected Outcome: Ventilator powers on without low battery alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Breathing Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Check that inspiratory and expiratory limbs are connected correctly. Ensure all filters, humidifiers, and adapters are installed as per protocol. Expected Outcome: No loose connections or misaligned components.

steps.title: Examine Flow and Pressure Sensors
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall flow and pressure sensors to ensure proper seating. Check for visible damage, debris, or condensation in sensor ports. Expected Outcome: Sensors are clean, aligned, and properly seated.

steps.title: Verify Exhalation Valve and PEEP Valve
steps.instructions: Inspect the exhalation valve and PEEP valve for obstruction or misalignment. Clean or reposition if necessary. Expected Outcome: Valves move freely without sticking or blockage.

steps.title: Run a Pre-Use Check Again
steps.instructions: Restart the ventilator and initiate the pre-use check. Observe for any alarms or errors during the test. Expected Outcome: Pre-use check completes successfully.

CCR.Complaint: Pre-use check fails to complete; ventilator displays error code 06.
CCR.Cause: Expiratory valve was misaligned, causing circuit detection errors.
CCR.Resolution: Exhalation valve realigned, sensors reseated, and pre-use check completed successfully.

helpfulDetails: Outlet verified with a multimeter or known device.
Flow and pressure sensors reinstalled and checked.
Valve inspection performed; no debris detected.
Pre-use check re-run; completed without errors.
Final device status: Ready for patient use.

title: CareFusion AVEA Ventilator - Internal Battery Degradation
description: This guide assists in identifying and managing internal battery degradation in the CareFusion AVEA. Battery issues can reduce transport runtime, prevent proper battery charging, and trigger low-battery alarms.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is not actively supporting a patient during testing. If the patient is on this device, transfer them to another ventilator to maintain uninterrupted ventilation.

steps.title: Check External Power and Charging
steps.instructions: Verify the AC power cord is fully connected and the outlet is functional. Observe the charging indicator on the ventilator. A healthy battery should indicate charging when plugged in.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Connection and Condition
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the internal battery following manufacturer guidelines. Check for visible damage, corrosion, or swelling of the battery.

steps.title: Run Battery Self-Test (If Available)
steps.instructions: Use the ventilator’s built-in battery test function. Record reported runtime and health status. Compare to expected specifications.

steps.title: Assess Runtime Performance
steps.instructions: Disconnect the ventilator from AC power and monitor how long it runs on battery alone. If runtime is significantly below specifications or the ventilator shuts down prematurely, this confirms battery degradation.

steps.title: Replace Battery If Necessary
steps.instructions: If degradation is confirmed, schedule internal battery replacement following manufacturer procedures. Use only approved CareFusion replacement batteries.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator battery does not hold charge; runtime is below specification.
CCR.Cause: Internal battery has degraded and cannot sustain proper operation.
CCR.Resolution: Battery replaced with manufacturer-approved unit; ventilator tested and returned to service.

title: CareFusion AVEA Ventilator - Compressor or Blower Failure
description: This guide assists in troubleshooting compressor or blower failures on the CareFusion AVEA. Failure of the compressor or blower can prevent proper gas delivery, trigger high-pressure or low-flow alarms, and compromise patient ventilation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to an alternative ventilator or manual ventilation before proceeding. Confirm alarms are silenced and do not interfere with patient care.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify the ventilator is connected to a live outlet or fully charged battery. Confirm the power switch is on and circuit breakers are not tripped. Expected outcome: Ventilator powers on without error codes.

steps.title: Inspect Air Supply and Connections
steps.instructions: Check all gas inlet lines for proper connection and no kinks or obstructions. Inspect the compressor or blower intake for dust, debris, or blockage. Expected outcome: Clear airflow path, no restriction to intake.

steps.title: Listen for Unusual Noises
steps.instructions: Turn on the ventilator and listen for abnormal motor sounds (grinding, squealing, or failure to spin). Expected outcome: Normal operational sound indicates external causes are less likely.

steps.title: Verify Settings and Alarms
steps.instructions: Check the ventilator display for specific blower/compressor error messages. Confirm that no settings inadvertently limit airflow (e.g., low tidal volume or high-pressure limits). Expected outcome: Error messages cleared if caused by misconfiguration.

steps.title: Swap External Components
steps.instructions: If a backup compressor or blower module is available, swap to confirm whether the issue is internal or external. Expected outcome: Device operates normally with a known good module, confirming internal component failure in the original module.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator failed to deliver adequate airflow; blower error alarm displayed.
CCR.Cause: Compressor motor not operating; no airflow detected despite proper power and connections.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench repair.

helpfulDetails: Gas lines and intake free of obstruction.
